mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% increase in industrial production over the past month signals a strengthening demand environment that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the unemployment rate over the past month signals a weakening labor market that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in weakening labor market periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 5% decline in housing starts over the past month signals a weakening supply environment that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in weakening supply periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% increase in real GDP over the past quarter signals a strengthening demand environment that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the federal funds rate over the past month signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in tightening monetary periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% increase in real GDP over the past quarter and a 5% increase in industrial production over the past month signal a strengthening demand environment that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the unemployment rate over the past month and a 5% increase in initial claims over the past week signal a weakening labor market that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in weakening labor market periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
